# Supabase Migration Squash Procedure

## Problem
When migrations are squashed into a baseline file (e.g., `20260422120108_v2.11.0-baseline.sql`), the remote database may still contain records of the old, pre-squash migration IDs in its `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations` table. 

Because these files no longer exist in the `supabase/migrations` directory, the Supabase CLI (and GitHub CI) will fail with:
`Remote migration versions not found in local migrations directory.`

## Solution: Migration Repair
We must synchronize the remote migration history with the local state using the `supabase migration repair` command.

### 1. Identify Missing IDs
The error message in CI or local `supabase db push` will list the IDs that exist on the remote but are missing locally.

### 2. Mark Baseline as Applied
The new baseline migration must be marked as `applied` on the remote so the CLI knows it doesn't need to run it (since the schema already reflects this state).

```bash
supabase migration repair --db-url "<DB_URL>" 20260422120108 --status applied
```

### 3. Mark Squashed IDs as Reverted
All IDs that were squashed into the baseline must be marked as `reverted`. This removes them from the remote migration tracking table without actually rolling back the schema changes they once performed.

```bash
# Example for multiple IDs
supabase migration repair --db-url "<DB_URL>" <ID_1> <ID_2> ... --status reverted
```

## Reference: May 2026 Squash
The following command repairs the state for the v2.11.0 baseline squash:

```bash
supabase migration repair --db-url "<PROD_DB_URL>" \
```
